skill.name: Legal Advisor
skill.description: Draft privacy policies, terms of service, disclaimers, and legal notices. Creates GDPR-compliant texts, cookie policies, and data processing agreements.
skill.summary: AI-powered legal documentation assistant for privacy policies, terms of service, and regulatory compliance.
skill.author: sickn33

security_audit.summary: Static analysis scanned 0 files with 0 lines, detecting 0 security issues. Risk score: 0/100. This is a prompt-only skill with no executable code, scripts, or network access. The skill provides legal documentation templates and compliance guidance through natural language prompts only.

content.value_statement: Creating legal documents for your business requires expertise in multiple regulations. This skill delivers GDPR, CCPA, and other compliant templates with clear language and mandatory disclosures.
actual_capabilities: Draft privacy policies compliant with GDPR, CCPA, and LGPD regulations
Create terms of service and user agreements for web and mobile applications
Generate cookie policies and consent management documentation
Produce data processing agreements (DPA) for vendor relationships
Write disclaimers, liability limitations, and intellectual property notices
Provide compliance checklists for multiple jurisdictions
limitations: Output is informational only and does not constitute legal advice
Cannot provide attorney-client privilege or legal representation
May not reflect the most recent regulatory changes without updates
Requires human review for complex or high-risk legal situations

use_cases.title: Startup Compliance Setup
use_cases.description: Generate foundational legal documents for a new SaaS product including privacy policy, terms of service, and cookie policy tailored to target markets.

use_cases.title: E-commerce Legal Requirements
use_cases.description: Create comprehensive legal documentation for online stores including refund policies, shipping terms, and consumer rights notices.

use_cases.title: GDPR Compliance Audit
use_cases.description: Review and update existing privacy policies to ensure compliance with European data protection requirements and mandatory disclosures.

prompt_templates.title: Basic Privacy Policy
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a privacy policy for a small blog that collects email addresses for newsletters. The blog is based in the US but has readers in the EU. Include basic GDPR disclosures and data retention information.

prompt_templates.title: SaaS Terms of Service
prompt_templates.prompt: Draft terms of service for a project management SaaS platform. Include sections on user accounts, payment terms, intellectual property, limitation of liability, and termination clauses. The service is available worldwide.

prompt_templates.title: Cookie Policy and Consent
prompt_templates.prompt: Write a cookie policy for an e-commerce site that uses analytics cookies, advertising cookies, and essential session cookies. Explain each type, their purpose, and how users can manage consent under the ePrivacy Directive.

prompt_templates.title: Data Processing Agreement
prompt_templates.prompt: Generate a data processing agreement between a company (data controller) and their cloud hosting provider (data processor). Include GDPR Article 28 requirements, security measures, subprocessor authorization, and data breach notification procedures.

output_examples.input: Privacy policy for a mobile fitness app
output_examples.output: A structured privacy policy document with sections covering data collection types (health metrics, location, usage data), legal basis for processing, user rights under GDPR and CCPA, data retention periods, third-party sharing disclosures, and contact information for privacy requests.

output_examples.input: Terms of service for freelance marketplace
output_examples.output: Complete terms including account eligibility, freelancer and client responsibilities, payment processing terms, dispute resolution procedures, intellectual property ownership of work product, prohibited activities, and platform liability limitations with jurisdiction-specific clauses.

best_practices: Always include clear placeholder markers for company-specific information that requires customization
Structure documents with descriptive headers to improve readability and user navigation
Provide jurisdiction-specific variations when serving multiple regulatory regions
anti_patterns: Using legal templates without customizing for your specific business model and data practices
Failing to update documents when regulations change or business practices evolve
Treating AI-generated legal content as a substitute for qualified attorney review on high-risk matters

faq.question: Is the output from this skill legally binding?
faq.answer: No. The skill provides templates for informational purposes only. Legal documents become binding when properly executed and customized for your situation. Always consult a qualified attorney for legal advice.

faq.question: Does this skill cover all jurisdictions worldwide?
faq.answer: The skill focuses on major regulations like GDPR, CCPA, LGPD, and PIPEDA. It provides jurisdiction-specific variations where possible, but local laws vary significantly. Regional legal review is recommended.

faq.question: How often should I update my legal documents?
faq.answer: Review legal documents at least annually, when regulations change, when your business practices evolve, or when entering new markets. The skill can help update existing documents to reflect new requirements.

faq.question: Can this skill create documents for regulated industries like healthcare or finance?
faq.answer: The skill can draft general templates, but highly regulated industries require specialized legal expertise. HIPAA, financial regulations, and sector-specific rules need attorney review and customization.

faq.question: What information do I need to provide for a custom privacy policy?
faq.answer: Provide your business location, data types collected, processing purposes, third-party services used, data retention periods, and target jurisdictions. More details produce more accurate and compliant documents.

faq.question: Does using this skill create attorney-client privilege?
faq.answer: No. AI tools do not create attorney-client relationships or privilege. Communications with this skill are not protected. Consult an attorney for privileged legal advice.
